item.title: I Can’t Draw, So I Love Low-Effort Tomodachi Life Custom Creations, Like This Single Pea On A Plate
item.content:encoded:
I am not good at drawing. I recognize that this is a skill I could, if I wanted, hone with practice. My issue is that I am impatient, and I do not want to take the time to make something look nice. So when faced with games like Tomodachi Life that rely on user-generated content and promote nearly boundless creativity, I tend to take the path of least resistance. When asked to decorate a slice of cake, I grab the whipped cream stamp and the single strawberry stamp, plop one of each on top, and dust off my hands. Job’s done.

It would be easy for me, in light of this, to go online and wither in the face of all the incredible, detailed designs other people are making in Tomodachi Life. You really can freely visually customize just about anything in this game. Miis are the most obvious, but you can create all manner of objects for them to play with: books, food, toys, pets. You can make custom tiles to decorate buildings and nature. Just poke your nose into the Tomodachi Life subreddit. The front page is riddled with ridiculous creations. As I write this, I’m looking at Hank Hill, a replicated Nirvana album cover, an excruciatingly detailed cat, and, uh…weed. A really detailed bag of weed.

But I’m not here to gush about any of those. I want to show you the incredible power of phoning it in:

That is a single circle of green on a plate, where the plate was provided by the game to put food on. The least possible effort someone could put in, short of doing nothing at all. And it’s also one of the funniest things I’ve seen in Tomodachi Life yet. Seeing this instantly filled me with renewed energy to visit the creator tool and make really god-awful art, and put it in my game.

This isn’t the only incredible low-effort doodle I’ve seen. I’m a big fan of this Hammer Dog, for instance. A little more effort than One Pea, but still a pretty quick-looking sketch that has incredible energy:

You can make signs in Tomodachi Life, which means some people are just using the text tool to make really simple but effective billboards like this recreated meme:

Here’s an example of someone who was clearly putting effort in, but wasn’t necessarily going the full distance: no elaborate shading, no heavy details, the yellow arches are a little wobbly. That’s fine! It’s a McDonald’s sign. It works great and looks nice:

The simple treatment also works for creating Miis. I’ve done a few real basic ones (including an Emily Dickinson that’s just the default feminine Mii with a hairstyle change), but these two are maybe the pinnacle of low-effort but still some effort:

One of the joys of Tomodachi Life is that, as above, sometimes the scenarios just line up perfectly with the thing you’ve created:

Just a couple more. I didn’t want to include too many low-effort creations that were clearly just someone importing images from something else, as the actual act of getting a Switch 2 to support that is a little on the high-effort side. But I respect the power behind just sticking screenshots of Unreal and Unity crashes into Tomodachi Life and personifying them like this:

I realize I’m veering close to high-effort now, but finally, please enjoy this upgrade to the Single Pea on a Plate: “Whole tray of peas.”

Tomodachi Life rules. If you’re avoiding it because you feel you can’t live up to the creative enterprises of people you don’t know and will never speak to, banish those fears now. Make a pea on a plate. Make messy, simple, low-effort art. Be free with me.



https://kotaku.com/low-effort-tomodachi-life-creations-pea-plate-2000689346

item.title: Edward Kenway Voice Actor Returns For Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced
item.content:encoded:
You ask many Assassin’s Creed fans who their favorite AC main character is, and they are likely to offer Edward Kenway as an answer, due to the sterling performance of Matt Ryan. Any concerns about the voice actor being replaced for Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced have been thrown out quicker than gone-off grog.

Ezio Auditore da Firenze might win the hearts of the majority of AC fans, as will Altair, but you’ve got to admit, Edward Kenway is a badass in his own right. There’s a reason so many Assassin’s Creed fans have been eager for a Black Flag Remake, and it’s good news for you, Edward Kenway fans.

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Casts Matt Ryan as Edward Kenway

Barely a minute into the leaked Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced presentation, and we’re given a heartwarming greeting by none other than Matt Ryan in the flesh.

Ryan is an accomplished actor across TV and movies, but many remember him as Edward Kenway in Assassin’s Creed Black Flag.

As with other Resynced stories we’ve revealed here at Insider Gaming, such as details on the Resynced Collector’s Edition Goodies, the Animus returning in the remake with new moments, and of course, confirmation that Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced doesn’t include DLC or Multiplayer, everything has come from a leaked Ubisoft presentation that was sent to us.

We are not at liberty to divulge images or videos from it, but rest assured that Matt Ryan effectively acts as a host and narrator for the showcase—even interacting with a couple of dev members to discuss Resynced’s development and changes!

There are even fascinating snippets of the actor mocapping for the game, and he’s recorded lots of new dialogue, as Resynced offers a lot of new content in addition to the base features you all remember.

Funnily enough, if you cast your mind back, Matt Ryan even hinted at an Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Remake back in June 2025, and the leaked video allegedly had Ubisoft threatening to sue the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag actor over the comment.

It’s fair to say it might not have been Black Flag without Matt Ryan. It’s excellent to know he’s back, so let us know if you’re also excited by leaving a comment on the news and game in general, through our free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord.

There’s so much more gaming news here at IG, including Call of Duty no longer releasing Day One on Xbox Game Pass and Xbox’s CEO announcing huge Game Pass Price Drops.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

item.title: New Dawn of War 4 Adeptus Mechanicus Trailer
item.content:encoded:
"I don't know whether to laugh or cry."

Upcoming RTS Dawn of War 4 has a new CGI trailer to reveal the Adeptus Mechanicus, but it was the depiction of Necrons that sparked a debate within the Warhammer 40,000 community.

The trailer acts as an introduction to the Adeptus Mechanicus, the Imperium-aligned faction within the Warhammer 40,000 universe that’s made up of cybernetically enhanced, technology-obsessed warrior priests who worship the Omnissiah, aka the Machine God.

Things start off well enough, with Magos Dominus Nulpherus-1 and a fellow tech priest descending into what they believe to be a long-buried Necron tomb while on the hunt for lost knowledge. As you’d expect, the Necrons wake up, and based on how well they perform out in the field here, they should have stayed asleep.

Here’s the official blurb on what happens next, courtesy of publisher Deep Silver and developer KING Art Games:

Necron forces begin to awaken, turning a calculated exploration into a chaotic firefight within the collapsing ruins. With the situation rapidly spiralling out of control, Magos Nulpherus-1 is forced into a fighting retreat.
Salvation comes in the form of Sicarian Ruststalkers, cutting through the darkness as the Adeptus Mechanicus make their escape. What starts as a mission of discovery quickly becomes a battle to survive.

The thing is, the Necrons are next to useless in this battle. They slowly move forward firing their xenos weapons with all the accuracy of a drunk Stormtrooper, conveniently taking it in turns to miss shot after shot. When they do get into melee range, they crumble like plastic toys. And yes, they eventually overwhelm their foe, but only after extreme, perhaps improbable losses against just two Adeptus Mechanicus.

It’s fair to say Warhammer 40,000 fans have picked up on this, with a stream of comments across the likes of YouTube, Warhammer 40,000 subreddits, and even IGN’s own comments calling out the depiction of the Necrons here.

“Dude, did these Necrons roll all 1's with 20 dice?” said one fan. “Those necrons got teleported from the Star Wars universe,” said another. “You know the new faction has plot armor when they are presented,” another fan commented. “I don't know whether to laugh or cry,” another said. “The strongest Tech Priest in all 40,000 years,” another joked. You get the idea.

For me, this Adeptus Mechanicus trailer isn’t the best we’ve seen board the Dawn of War 4 hype train. Previous trailers focused on the Space Marines were much better, and even confirmed some pretty exciting gameplay features. But this is just a CGI trailer after all. It’s not gameplay, and that’s what really counts.

Dawn of War 4 is still without a release date, but we can expect a release on PC at some point this year. fingers crossed the next faction reveal trailer isn't so one sided.

Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.

Recommends

item.title: BECROWNED launches this fall
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Psychological survival horror game gets a release window.

Psychological survival horror game BECROWNED will launch for PC via Steam this fall, publisher Top Hat Studios and developer 13th Street Games announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

A surreal survival horror set in a world where fears come alive, and danger lurks at every turn. Explore the ruins of an ancient kingdom, fight monsters, and try to survive in this brutal world. Will you become its savior, or will you seek to destroy it? The choice is yours.

Key Features

  • The World Woven From the Nightmares of a Distant Past – Sinners, heretics, and even gods—this merciless, twisted world is a prison for those who truly deserve punishment. Face their deepest fears and nightmares as this world brings them to life. In this place, there are no innocents, no kindness, and no mercy. Will you prove this truth or will you challenge it?
  • Classic Survival Horror – Explore locations filled with secrets. Solve puzzles, manage your resources wisely, and avoid unnecessary fights. Who knows what terrible creature might be lurking around the next corner…
  • A Drama About a Man Who Became a Prisoner of His Own Ambitions – Richard Torrance is a man who will do anything to get what he wants. Не is a man of strong will and unbreakable character. He is rational and cold-blooded. Richard strives to shape the world to his liking. Despite his efforts, he has never achieved what he truly desires – happiness. What led him to a world of endless suffering? Uncover the mysteries of the protagonist’s past to find out.
  • Varied Gameplay – Explore a vast castle and its surroundings, discover hidden passages and optional puzzles. Battle a variety of enemies, each requiring a unique fighting strategy, and defeat challenging bosses. Survive this nightmare. No matter the cost!
  • A Nonlinear Storyline and a Living World That Responds to the Player’s Choices – You will face countless choices, each impacting the fates of others imprisoned in this world. Will you spare a repentant killer? Will you risk your resources to save a random martyr from a gruesome death? Is it worth the cost? You will suffer the consequences. The game features multiple endings, ranging from happy to a true nightmare. Will you walk the path of a hero, or just kill ’em all? The choice is yours.
  • Technologically Advanced Retro Style – Modern technology, blended with a distinct retro style, creates captivating visuals that convey both the horrors and the beauty of the game’s world.

Watch a new trailer below.

A Kingdom by the Sea Trailer

item.title: Twinkle’s Galactic Tour ‘Wildest Dream’ trailer
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Latest look at the PS1-style retro tribute game.

Publisher Top Hat Studios and developer Molegato have released a new trailer for 3D platformer Twinkle’s Galactic Tour dubbed “Wildest Dream.”

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Travel the galaxy to rescue Twinkle’s debut concert’s audience in a low-poly, 90s anime styled, musical platforming adventure!

Play each level to the beat of a song as enemies, platforms and items all move following the beat, the melody or the drums. Uncover musical minigames or explore the personal story of an struggling (but never surrendering!) young aspirant singer.

Galaxy Round Tour

Explore neon-and-pastel alien cities, travel on Twinkle’s best friend and roadie Bav’s space van, help the locals by completing missions, sample the local cuisine or shop around for useful gear or fashionable costumes!

Collect Everything

Optional objectives in levels like finishing one try, time trials or finding the hidden CDs will grant you materials to craft power ups and tools. Find lots of secondary missions and hidden items in the open cities and return to previous areas once you’ve got new tools to reach new places!

Experience Twinkle’s and each character’s narratives through completing missions and the ‘hangout system’, in a story that is all about what it feels trying to make it out in life as an artist.

Loving the Art

Will Twinkle catch the culprit and serve justice? Find out her footing in the harsh career of music? Which friends will you choose to make? Maybe even find your love in the midst of it all?

So be a star and help Twinkle and her friends save everyone beat after beat, song after song!

Twinkle’s Galactic Tour will be available for PC via Steam. A release date has yet to be announced.

Watch the trailer below.

Wildest Dream Trailer

item.content:encoded:
• PC

Story-rich horror RPG due out in 2026.

A demo for The Backworld, a turn-based RPG with narrative-driven horror elements, is now available for PC via Steam, publisher Top Hat Studios and developer Numor Games announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Top Hat Studios:

A surreal, story-rich, horror RPG, The Backworld plunges players into a cosmic adventure where reality is crumbling. As you slip between dimensions, you’ll explore over 100 diverse areas ranging from terrifying, liminal spaces to traditional JRPG-inspired fantasy worlds to horrors beyond your wildest imagination. Survival requires strategic decision-making; whether that means engaging in combat, sneaking past threats, or utilizing environmental hazards is up to you. Along the way, keep an eye on your sanity; your experiences with increasingly surreal horrors will affect your sanity and dynamically alter gameplay, causing characters to lose control or the player to experience hallucinations such as fake NPCs and user interface glitches.

The Backworld is due out for PC via Steam in 2026.

item.title: Cel-shaded aerial roguelike action game DELIVERY MUST COMPLETE to be published by Top Hat Studios
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Deliver a package through the heart of a chaotic warzone.

Top Hat Studios will publish Yoyoyollie-developed cel-shaded aerial roguelike action game DELIVERY MUST COMPLETE, the companies announced. It will be available for PC via Steam.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

DELIVERY MUST COMPLETE—Devil May Cry meets Ace Combat. a high speed Ace-like roguelike with SSSTYLE. Cut across cel-shaded, action-packed, arcade-paced dogfights as you fly, fight, and upgrade your roster of kickass planes. Every battle is massive, every location is procedural, and every fight is a spectacle—whether it’s aircraft, blimps, dragons or mechs.

Be Stylish, Be a Spectacle, Be an Ace

Weave, destroy, dodge, and parry everything that comes your way. Earn style points and enhance your style ranking by flying like a true ace. The stage is all yours, show the enemy your moves!

A Different Sky Each Time

Every map is procedurally generated and no two fights are alike: Weather changes, cloud cover variation, terrain anomalies, and random map events among four distinct environmental zones change the battlefield each time you play.

Burn Through the Skies

Make sharp turns and aggressive plays by Drifting, because angle of attack is just a suggestion to you. Boost forward and cut through clouds, leaving everything in the dust. No, it’s not your plane. You are just built different.

Complete Your Delivery

You’re the Courier.

Your mission? Deliver the package.

The destination? Right in the middle of an all-out warzone, where elite fighter squadrons from across the globe swarm the sky all united for the noble goal of glory and peace… with only you to get in their way, again and again and again.

Crashing and burning is just a temporary inconvenience for you, however: Warp tech will pull you back.

No matter how far you fly, no matter how long it takes, no matter how many elite pilots they send after you, nothing will stop you from finishing your goal.

The DELIVERY MUST COMPLETE. (Hopefully before it gets cold!)

Watch a new trailer below.

Publisher Announce Trailer
